summary: Completed testing of Infrastructure Management feature - all APIs and UI components working correctly. Login with admin@test.com/admin123 works. Infrastructure CRUD APIs verified. Admin page shows Infrastructure Monitoring card with working 'Manage Devices' button. Sidebar correctly shows Infrastructure, Admin, and Settings in admin section for admin users.
passed_tests: Login with admin@test.com / admin123 works (200 OK)
POST /api/infrastructure/devices - creates ping device with correct fields
POST /api/infrastructure/devices - creates Proxmox device with port 8006
POST /api/infrastructure/devices - creates SNMP device with community string
DELETE /api/infrastructure/devices/{id} - deletes device and verifies removal
PUT /api/infrastructure/devices/{id} - updates device name, IP, location
GET /api/zammad/stats - endpoint exists (external service not available in preview)
Admin user role verified - user sees Infrastructure, Admin, Settings in sidebar

infrastructure_page.heading: Infrastructure Monitoring
infrastructure_page.description: Monitor Proxmox hosts, routers, and other network devices

device_types_supported.proxmox: Proxmox API with token auth (port 8006 default)
device_types_supported.snmp: SNMP v1/v2c with community string (port 161 default)

updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_infrastructure.py - New test file for Infrastructure Management

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Infrastructure page, Admin page, sidebar navigation all verified)

seed_data_creation: None - tests use existing admin@test.com account and clean up test devices after each run
context_for_next_testing_agent: Infrastructure Management feature fully implemented and tested. CRUD APIs at /api/infrastructure/devices work correctly. Infrastructure page at /infrastructure renders with status summary cards and device table. Admin page has Infrastructure Monitoring card with 'Manage Devices' button that navigates to /infrastructure. Sidebar shows Infrastructure link in admin section for admin users. Zammad stats endpoint exists but external service not available in preview environment - this is expected.

notes.zammad_integration: Zammad stats endpoint exists and returns 200 when connected. External Zammad service not available in preview environment, which is expected behavior.
notes.external_integrations: TRMM, Bitdefender, Zammad require connection to user's internal network - cannot be fully tested in preview
